Crime drama, starring David Caruso, Adam Rodriguez and Khandi Alexander(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony tries to piece together the events that led to him lying wounded in a hospital bed, having been shot in the chest through his bulletproof vest. programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Sam's wife Michelle resumes her CIA cover to investigate a hit on a Russian mob boss with possible links to Sidorov and the stolen nukes. programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: In a special edition of the detective drama that features only two members of the team, Gerry Standing and Steve McAndrew travel to Glasgow, where a new Ucos section is being set up. While there, they agree to assist the investigation into the 1993 unsolved murder of James Soutar, the wealthy owner of a string of betting shops. Cathy Sinclair, one of the beneficiaries of the bookie's will at his death, had a care-home upbringing in common with Soutar, but she claims to have no idea who he was or why she was left £15,000. McAndrew has a personal stake in the case - the original investigating officer was corrupt cop Frank McNair, who had an affair with his wife, and their paths soon cross. The London-based officers' questions begin to unsettle someone and a campaign of victimisation against them begins, but it's a revelation from McAndrew's girlfriend Charley that reawakens a long-dormant scandal and clears the path to a solution. Dennis Waterman and Denis Lawson star(n)

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Frank Skinner invites satirist Charlie Brooker, presenter Scarlett Moffatt and actress Pearl Mackie to express their anger over individuals, events and objects that upset them, hoping to curry the host's favour and see them consigned to oblivion. Their gripes include crocodiles, mosquitoes and having one's hair cut(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: As Prince Andrew steps back from his public duties, Darragh MacIntyre hears from the victims of is former friend and convicted child sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. The Duke of York has been widely criticised for not expressing sympathy for these women during his television interview with the BBC's Newsnight programme. Virginia Roberts Giuffre claims she was trafficked by Epstein and forced to have sex with the prince, something he denies. In her first interview on British TV, she tells her story and reveals new details about her time with Epstein. Reporter Darragh MacIntyre also looks at another of the duke's friends, Ghislaine Maxwell, and investigates her alleged role in Epstein's prolific sex offending. As the FBI investigation continues, Panorama asks what next in the scandal of Epstein and the prince?(n)

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor and Jock and Mairead come up with a scheme to make money and buy a second-hand chip van, persuading Mairead to come on board with the venture on the understanding that she will provide the fish. huge football match in Cork provides them with an opportunity to make a killing financially, but luck doesn't always go their way(n)

programme.desc: Guests include US politician Hillary Clinton and her daughter Chelsea, co-authors of The Book of Gutsy Women. programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Adam helps Joe get through a traumatic event. Meanwhile, Sid demonstrates his marketing skills, and a father forces his daughter to confront her feelings of guilt(n)

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Max and Miranda investigate the brutal murder of the famous British author Nicholas Mountford, and investigate the possible link to a bull fighting ban(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie and the Rich brothers try to come up with a contemporary cottage garden design in Norwich to please Jane and Terry, who are unhappy with their existing back garden(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Three treasured family heirlooms are brought back to life, a vintage bottle stopper, a cherished peddle scooter, and an engraved powder compact(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: An election broadcast by the Green Party(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Matt Baker(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Neil Interviews
programme.desc: The broadcaster interviews Jo Swinson, leader of the Liberal Democrats(n)

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Sheriffs Dave Steele and Michael Perkins visit a man who owes his ex-wife thousands of pounds, leaving her on the verge of bankruptcy. Meanwhile, Rob and Gerald visit a beautiful country house in search of the money owed to a wedding planner who has not received payment, and Billy and Claire challenge the owners of a car dealership(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: As the tenth week of the competition begins, Lord sugar summons the candidates to Burlington House in Central London. Here, the remaining candidates are informed that for this week's task, they have been challenged to create and sell their own perfume, coming up with their own signature scent and design to sell to major retailers. For one team a failure to make decisions proves problematic, while on the other team a bold attempt to make their fragrance stand out does not go to plan. Back in the boardroom, one of the candidates is sent home from the competition(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the midweek Premier League matches, including Liverpool v Everton at Anfield and Manchester United v Tottenham Hotspur at Old Trafford. The Reds went into this match looking to extend their unbeaten run against their Merseyside rivals to 20 matches and further strengthen their title challenge in the process. Meanwhile, United and Spurs were both looking to kickstart their campaigns after disappointing starts to the season. Presented by Gary Lineker, with analysis by Danny Murphy and Jermaine Jenas(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein examine the 1935 morphine poisoning of a care home resident Ada Baguley in Nottingham. Initially Ada was thought to have died from natural causes, but a post-mortem revealed her body to contain lethal doses of morphine. Dorothea Waddingham, the owner of the nursing home and primary care giver of Ada, was later convicted of murder(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metal expert Dom Chinea and wood restorer Will Kirk team up to tackle the restoration of a treasured steering wheel from a scrapped Scottish fishing boat. A worn-out Portsmouth football club mascot and a vintage cricket ball are also revived. Presented by Jay Blades(n)

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: It's been raining for days and vet Maurice King is called into the practice car park to examine a newborn lamb that's in danger of succumbing to hypothermia. The birthing season in the Mournes is reaching its peak as Rachael Frew determines a heavily pregnant ewe must undergo an emergency C-section. Meanwhile, Flame the dachshund and Monty the fell pony present worrying symptoms that put the vets' knowledge to the test. Narrated by Kevin Murphy(n)

programme.desc: Coverage of the sixth day at the Barbican Centre in York, featuring best-of-11-frame matches in the last 32 of the prestigious ranking tournament. Eventual champion Ronnie O'Sullivan was at his unstoppable best at this stage last year, whitewashing Zhou Yuelong 6-0. Hazel Irvine presents, with commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus, Dennis Taylor and John Virgo(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea

The contestants must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of Green iguana, Eurasian lynx, American bison and Western gorilla(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second round of the student quiz continues, with two more teams of four pitting their wits against one another as they attempt to secure a place in the quarter-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: Vienna Blood
programme.desc: As Max tells his family that Clara has broken off their engagement, the Liebermanns are shocked by an act of violence committed by Leah's son Daniel. When bruises are found on the boy's body, Max persuades Oskar that the military academy his nephew attends should be investigated. The learn that one of the pupils died in an accident 10 days previously and when Max finds a coded note in Daniel's things he begins to suspect some sinister goings on at the academy. Crime drama, starring Matthew Beard(n)

programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents a Question Time Leaders Special from Peterborough with the leader of the Brexit Party, Nigel Farage. Thirty minutes of questions and debate from a selected audience as part of a series of special election programmes on the BBC(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: In the fourth week of the competition, the candidates are challenged to create their own range of electric bikes, before hosting their own launch event to sell their bikes to some of the UK's biggest cycle retailers. As the task unfolds, half of the team head to Nottingham to develop their new bike, while the other half of the team stay in London to choose accessories and create marketing material. At their events, poor pricing strategies and forceful tactics prove problematic, and back in the boardroom, one candidate is sent home(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker heads to the heart of the Snowdonia National Park to explore its Slate Trail, an 83-mile loop taking in some of the area's least-trodden path, while Ellie Harrison is in the foothills of Llanberis to find about about the Outdoor Partnership. Plus, Tom Heap investigates the impact of falling farm gate prices on the British beef industry(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Kian is desperate to deliver the surgery that Bea desires, but Jac is too stressed and refuses to help, forcing him to consider what he is willing to sacrifice to give her what she wants. Dom and Lofty are too busy putting others first to deal with their own problems, and Donna distracts herself from her troubles by focussing on a returning patient. But when she goes missing, Donna has to be brave enough to convince them both to face their fears. Guest starring Lorraine Chase (Emmerdale)(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass examine the violent assault and murder of a teenage girl in 1918, and how a button and badge found near her body led to the arrest of an ex-serviceman(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Furniture experts, horologists, metal workers, ceramicists and upholsterers try to restore family heirlooms and treasured possessions to their former glory(n)

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo crosses the south east of Australia from Canberra, the seat of government, to the multi-ethnic heart of its second largest city, Melbourne. Along the way he discovers the history of the Ashes at Melbourne cricket ground, learns about the exploits of outlaw Ned Kelly and visits the home of superstar soprano Dame Nellie Melba. He also rides on the Puffing Billy narrow gauge steam railway, built in 1900, and has a go at shearing a sheep(n)

The only match to go to a deciding frame at this stage last year saw two-time champion Neil Robertson edge past 2006 world title-winner Graeme Dott. Hazel Irvine presents, with commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus, Dennis Taylor and John Virgo(n)

programme.desc: James Lewis and Jonathan Pratt are in Glemham Hall, Suffolk, where they uncover a Victorian peddler doll complete with wicker basket, while Paul Martin explores Framlingham Castle(n)

programme.desc: Rick Edwards, Scarlett Moffatt, Naga Munchetty and David O'Doherty test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with a winner declared on Friday(n)

programme.desc: Gemma Oaten and Samantha Renke hunt for antiques in Lancashire, accompanied by experts Philip Serrell and James Braxton. They discover the history of Blackpool's tram network and Lancaster's Grand Theatre, while a donkey makes a surprise appearance at the auction in Kendal(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace hosts the first round of Knockout week, as the 12 chefs to make it through the finals compete to stay in the contest. The chefs face an invention test in which they must not use any modern kitchen gadgets. The four weakest performing contestants then go through to a cook-off, to decide which two will be eliminated from the competition(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: Rick's journey takes him to the Spanish border and the Mediterranean coast. He discovers how Catalan and French influences have shaped the culinary legacy of Roussilon in the foothills of the Pyrenees, and samples some of the area's famous mountain cuisine, including grilled sausages, snails and fresh fish caught in a self-contained seawater lake - all with generous servings of the region's famous garlic mayonnaise. Back in his country kitchen, Rick prepares pork and beef meatballs in a tomato and piment sauce(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Ardal O'Hanlon plays host in this edition of the comedy showcase, recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo. The show features sets by Mawaan Rizwan and Sara Barron(n)

programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.desc: Jonathan Bartley, Co-Leader of the Green Party, takes questions from a studio audience about the party's policy platform for the general election. Presented by Victoria Derbyshire(n)

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Warship
programme.desc: After three months at sea, the test pilots from the Royal Navy and RAF have successfully landed the F35 jets on the flight deck of HMS Queen Elizabeth for the first time, and even tested launching and landing in hurricane conditions. But with just a few weeks to go before the warship arrives in New York in spectacular style, they are about to try a method of landing never attempted before, which carries great risks but could revolutionise carrier operations globally. Narrated by Caroline Catz(n)

programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste investigate a scam that saw its unwitting victim conned out of his life savings and dragged into a money-laundering ring(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Furniture experts, horologists, metal workers, ceramicists and upholsterers try to restore family heirlooms and treasured possessions to their former glory(n)

programme.desc: Looking at ways to reduce waste and save money by avoiding Christmas excess. Plus, a round up of the year in news and entertainment. With voiceover(n)

programme.desc: Samantha Evans and Shauna Guinn celebrate Cardiff's Gujarati community with a vegetarian cook-out, building a special giant multi-layered grill that several people can use at once to cook all the vegetables. The duo learn how to make an avocado and mango chutney using a recipe that has been handed down through the generations(n)

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the eighth day at the Barbican Centre in York, featuring best-of-11-frame matches in the last 16 of the prestigious ranking tournament. Ronnie O'Sullivan continued his march to the title at this stage last year with a 6-1 victory over Jack Lisowski. Hazel Irvine presents, with commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus, Dennis Taylor and John Virgo(n)

programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Previous winners Rick Edwards, Scarlett Moffatt, Naga Munchetty and David O'Doherty test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball presents more backstage gossip and exclusive news from Strictly Come Dancing. Plus, Ian Waite casts his eye over the couples' latest training footage(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Stars of stage and screen John Partridge and Anita Harris team up with antiques experts Margie Cooper and Paul Laidlaw for a Road Trip around Lancashire and Yorkshire. John struggles with how best to invest his £400, but decides to go with his instinct when he spies something theatrical, while Christmas comes early for Anita when she finds a ‘surprise' in a cabinet(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: It's Knockout Week, and with only ten chefs remaining in the competition, the first group of hopefuls are set two daunting challenges by Monica and Marcus. In the brand-new Pop Up Challenge, the chefs have to show off their versatility and style, creating and serving a dish that will win over the judges and the specially-invited guests. Back in the MasterChef kitchen, the chefs are pitted against each other in a cook-off to secure a place in the Semi-Finals(n)

programme.title: The Baby Has Landed
programme.desc: Documentary series following families throughout their first weeks of parenthood, lifting the lid on one of the most joyful, yet intense, times in a family's life. In the second episode, cameras follow new parents Mo and Syler as they get to grips with round-the-clock feeding and sleep deprivation, and Paul and Craig's surrogate Mel gives birth to their twins. Elsewhere, five-year-old Ezrah and seven-year-old Elijah have to come to terms with new rules in the family home after the birth of their baby sister(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice: You're Fired
programme.desc: The companion programme to The Apprentice, with Tom Allen talking to the tenth candidate to be ejected from the competition. The host is also joined by a panel of experts and fans, who offer an entertaining analysis of this week's task(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Rob Walker presents action from this evening's last-16 matches on the eighth day at the Barbican Centre in York(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Yorkshire Walks
programme.desc: Artist Shanaz Gulzar seeks inspiration as she explores Wensleydale, setting out from Leyburn on a walk to Bolton Castle, where Mary, Queen of Scots was imprisoned in 1568. Along the way, Shanaz discovers evidence of an industrial past and talks to a volunteer worker on the heritage railway line, while also stopping to sketch and reflect(n)

programme.title: Vienna: Empire, Dynasty and Dream
programme.desc: Simon Sebag Montefiore examines the transformation of Vienna into a great cultural capital after the Ottoman threat receded at the end of the 17th century. The city, no longer an outpost defending the West from Islamic invaders, was rebuilt and the Habsburgs vowed their empire and imperial capital would become the most glittering in the world, a magnet for musicians including Mozart, Haydn and Beethoven(n)

programme.title: Pleasure and Pain with Michael Mosley
programme.desc: The journalist examines why pleasure and pain are integral to human survival, submitting himself to some of the most extreme forms of both sensations. He reveals how the hottest chilli in the world creates a sense of euphoria in the brain, and explains the biological reasons why parents feel a surge of love for newborn children. He also exposes himself to painful experiments, highlighting the dangers of life without discomfort and discovering how far he is prepared to go to experience one of the world's most pleasurable highs(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Filmed over six years, this documentary reveals the struggle of victims of Spain's 40-year dictatorship under General Franco. It follows them as they organise a groundbreaking international lawsuit and fight a government- sanctioned 'pact of forgetting' relating to the crimes they suffered(n)

programme.title: A Timewatch Guide
programme.desc: Series examining how the BBC has revealed and interpreted moments in history, as well as looking at changes in research covered in documentary television. In the first edition, Professor Alice Roberts uses 70 years of BBC history archive to uncover how Stonehenge has been explained, argued over and debated by some of Britain's leading historians and archaeologists. She looks at how new theories discredited old ones, how astronomers and geologists became involved, and why, even after centuries of study, there is still no definitive answer to the mystery of Stonehenge(n)

programme.title: The Art of Japanese Life
programme.desc: James Fox explores how the art and culture produced by cities past and present has shaped the country's attitudes and helped forge the very idea of Japan itself(n)

programme.title: Vienna: Empire, Dynasty and Dream
programme.desc: Simon Sebag Montefiore examines the transformation of Vienna into a great cultural capital after the Ottoman threat receded at the end of the 17th century. The city, no longer an outpost defending the West from Islamic invaders, was rebuilt and the Habsburgs vowed their empire and imperial capital would become the most glittering in the world, a magnet for musicians including Mozart, Haydn and Beethoven(n)

programme.title: Pleasure and Pain with Michael Mosley
programme.desc: The journalist examines why pleasure and pain are integral to human survival, submitting himself to some of the most extreme forms of both sensations. He reveals how the hottest chilli in the world creates a sense of euphoria in the brain, and explains the biological reasons why parents feel a surge of love for newborn children. He also exposes himself to painful experiments, highlighting the dangers of life without discomfort and discovering how far he is prepared to go to experience one of the world's most pleasurable highs(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Yorkshire Walks
programme.desc: Artist Shanaz Gulzar goes on a ramble that straddles the north and south sides of Calderdale, stopping along the way to sketch and read poetry. She begins in the village of Heptonstall, rambles past Hell Hole and along the canal in Hebden Bridge, before climbing to the Stoodley Pike peace monument(n)

programme.title: Secret Life of Farm Animals
programme.desc: The documentary examining the behaviour of farm animals follows the first 12 weeks in the life of a Hereford calf, as it makes friends and settles into the herd. The films reveals how cows are highly social animals with complex personalities. They are also brilliant problem solvers with a love of music. Plus, how many clucks chickens use to communicate(n)

programme.title: Takaya: Lone Wolf
programme.desc: Documentary following wildlife photographer Cheryl Alexander's seven years documenting the life of a lone wolf on an island off the coast of British Columbia, Canada. The programme explores how the animal came to live alone on the island and adapted to his its new home, while Cheryl consults with leading experts to understand the solitary animal's behaviour(n)

programme.title: Koko: The Gorilla Who Talks to People
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the story of the only 'talking' gorilla in the world, Koko, and her lifelong relationship with animal psychologist Penny Patterson. Project Koko began as a PhD project to teach sign language to a baby gorilla, but an intense bond formed between the pair, and they have now been together for over 40 years. More than 2,000 hours of footage chart the most dramatic moments of Koko's unique life with Penny, from the battle to prevent the gorilla being taken back to the zoo in which she was born, to a clash with academic critics(n)

programme.title: The Turner Prize
programme.desc: Live coverage of the prestigious arts award from Dreamland Margate(n)

programme.title: Looking for Rembrandt
programme.desc: The painter's story is told from an unusual perspective, his own, beginning with his arrival in Amsterdam and his time courted by the city's wealthy elite. The programme examines his marriage and declining reputation for unreliability and financial irresponsibility. As he faces a series of personal tragedies, he also produces some of his most important works(n)

programme.title: Masters of the Pacific Coast: The Tribes of the American Northwest
programme.desc: Part one of two. Archaeologist Dr Jago Cooper explores the American Northwest, revealing how culture was established on the coast that has lasted for over 10,000 years. He travels to southeast Alaska, British Columbia and Washington State to see how a complex society developed without agriculture, and discloses the hidden significance in totem poles, canoes and intricate textiles(n)

programme.title: Revolution and Romance: Musical Masters of the 19th Century
programme.desc: Suzy Klein investigates how music became a powerful force for Europe's citizens during the French Revolution. She reveals how music came to express not only revolutionary fervour, but also inspired a growing sense of nationalism that was sweeping the continent. Chopin's music, for example, expressed the defiant spirit of the Polish population suffering oppression under a foreign power(n)

programme.title: Takaya: Lone Wolf
programme.desc: Documentary following wildlife photographer Cheryl Alexander's seven years documenting the life of a lone wolf on an island off the coast of British Columbia, Canada. The programme explores how the animal came to live alone on the island and adapted to his its new home, while Cheryl consults with leading experts to understand the solitary animal's behaviour(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Yorkshire Walks
programme.desc: Artist Shanaz Gulzar seeks inspiration while walking a stretch of the 109-mile Cleveland Way, meeting fellow walkers along the way and finding spots to sketch and recite poetry. Starting in Runswick Bay, and armed only with a handheld 360-degree selfie-style camera. she climbs the steep steps to the cliff top before strolling along the coastal path, absorbing the beautiful landscape and its industrial past, before finishing at Whitby for sunset(n)

programme.title: Sacred Wonders of Britain
programme.desc: Neil Oliver travels to Iona in the Inner Hebrides to learn about St Columba's tough brand of monasticism, and finds out how the epic journey of St Cuthbert led to the writing of the Lindisfarne Gospels and the building of Durham Cathedral. At Canterbury Cathedral, Neil discovers how the gruesome murder of Thomas Becket spurred the building of its nave, and on Glastonbury Tor in Somerset, he investigates the story of the Holy Grail, the sacred cup of everlasting life(n)

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: This edition looks at how a lobster led archaeologists to the discovery of an 8000-year-old Neolithic underwater settlement. Meanwhile, a team at a secret location in Dorset excavate an Iron Age crouched burial, and Naoise Mac Sweeney visits a modern construction site as it gives up the secrets of an Elizabethan playhouse(n)

programme.title: Vic & Bob's Big Night Out
programme.desc: Comedy series with Vic Reeves and Bob Mortimer, featuring a welcome return for Novelty Island, a special rendition of That's Magic, as well as an appearance from Michael Portillo as you have never seen him before(n)

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Cameras follow old friends Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer as they continue on their angling trip round the UK. In the Monsal Valley in Derbyshire, Paul tries to teach Bob how to fly fish for wild rainbow trout, and the pair reflect on their humble beginnings, their careers and their comedy contemporaries. Later, Bob cooks Paul his very personal recipe of tuna melanie on the river bank, and it soon transpires his cooking is a lot more successful than his fishing(n)

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Cameras follow old friends Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer as they continue on their angling trip round the UK. This time, Paul and Bob fish the river Wensum in Norfolk for Roach, but as the pair struggle up and down the riverbanks, they begin to realise how age is starting to take its toll on their physicality. After staying overnight in a disused train station and visiting a local pub, the pair decide to challenge each other to a fishing competition the following day, where the winner gets a very special culinary treat(n)

programme.title: Thailand: Earth's Tropical Paradise
programme.desc: Exploring the wildlife and culture of the Southeast Asian nation. The first episode examines the south of the country, a popular tourist destination for Westerners, and examines the role of spirituality in daily life. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo(n)

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Buster and Ladonna bond over a memorable trip to the movies, prompting Arthur to suspect Ladonna has taken his place as Buster's best bud(n)

programme.title: Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed!
programme.desc: Pieface laments that Paul is looking peaky and needs a holiday. Dennis has a plan - but it is only when Walter kidnaps Paul that he realises his chips are down and he needs the gang's help to put things right(n)

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: When the Baron stops the earth from spinning with a plague of traffic lights, only Danger Mouse and Penfold can save the world with a hotwired satellite and a moon-sized image of the Baron's green face(n)

programme.title: MOTD Kickabout
programme.desc: A look back at the weekend's football fixtures(n)

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Current affairs reports aimed at a younger audience(n)

It's too late to get a letter to the North Pole, so the Furchesters try to distract all the guests with their Christmas activities. However, every mention of Santa makes everyone worried again, they don't finish any of the activities they started. And Phoebe is disappointed that she can't enjoy her Christmas the way the Furchesters planned it. programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: As Prince Andrew steps back from his public duties, Darragh MacIntyre hears from the victims of is former friend and convicted child sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. The Duke of York has been widely criticised for not expressing sympathy for these women during his television interview with the BBC's Newsnight programme. Virginia Roberts Giuffre claims she was trafficked by Epstein and forced to have sex with the prince, something he denies. In her first interview on British TV, she tells her story and reveals new details about her time with Epstein. Reporter Darragh MacIntyre also looks at another of the duke's friends, Ghislaine Maxwell, and investigates her alleged role in Epstein's prolific sex offending. As the FBI investigation continues, Panorama asks what next in the scandal of Epstein and the prince?(n)

programme.title: Desperate to Drive
programme.desc: High risk drivers are tested at a unique centre in Cardiff, hoping to prove they are safe enough to get back behind the wheel after losing their licence. The programme follows the fortunes of four drivers: mum-of-four Fand who had to surrender her licence after a debilitating stroke, former lorry driver Glyn who has been diagnosed with dementia, Huw who lost a leg following a life threatening infection, and 17-year-old Emily who lives with cerebral palsy(n)

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Frank Skinner invites satirist Charlie Brooker, presenter Scarlett Moffatt and actress Pearl Mackie to express their anger over individuals, events and objects that upset them, hoping to curry the host's favour and see them consigned to oblivion. programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: As the tenth week of the competition begins, Lord sugar summons the candidates to Burlington House in Central London. programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The day of Mel's funeral arrives and everyone gathers to remember their friend - although Lisa's mood darkens when Phil and Sharon turn up at the wake. But when Lisa tries to confront the woman she blames for her friend's death, she is stopped by Louise. Chantelle is stunned when Gray opens up about his past to the therapist, Billy hatches a plan to solve Karen's money problems and Mick thinks of a new way to curb Linda's drinking(n)

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: A look at the troubled milk industry, while Anne Lundon counts mountain hares, and Nick Nairn and Dougie Vipond cook up a hearty soup in the food van(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: As Prince Andrew steps back from his public duties, Darragh MacIntyre hears from the victims of is former friend and convicted child sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. The Duke of York has been widely criticised for not expressing sympathy for these women during his television interview with the BBC's Newsnight programme. Virginia Roberts Giuffre claims she was trafficked by Epstein and forced to have sex with the prince, something he denies. In her first interview on British TV, she tells her story and reveals new details about her time with Epstein. Reporter Darragh MacIntyre also looks at another of the duke's friends, Ghislaine Maxwell, and investigates her alleged role in Epstein's prolific sex offending. As the FBI investigation continues, Panorama asks what next in the scandal of Epstein and the prince?(n)

programme.title: The Campaign
programme.desc: Rebecca Curran and Martin Geissler explain and analyse the latest news from the General Election campaign trail(n)

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Following Cheshire police's search for Mark Smith, an ex-soldier with PTSD who went missing after posting on social media that he wanted to end it all. Sgt Paul Chadwick assesses the case and is immediately concerned that a trained soldier in such a volatile state could pose a high risk - especially as Mark has previously stated that he would like to provoke police to shoot him. Narrated by Indira Varma(n)

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor and Jock and Mairead come up with a scheme to make money and buy a second-hand chip van, persuading Mairead to come on board with the venture on the understanding that she will provide the fish. huge football match in Cork provides them with an opportunity to make a killing financially, but luck doesn't always go their way(n)

programme.title: Gold Digger
programme.desc: Julia decides not to tell her children she is engaged when they visit at Christmas, leaving Benjamin feeling rejected. However, she ends up letting it slip over dinner - and none of her children are happy for her. Ted angrily questions Benjamin's motives, and cynically proposes to Marsha in an act of oneupmanship. programme.desc: The story of the amateur cowboy films made by Falkirk factory workers in the 1970s, and the impact that these films has on their community. programme.desc: Elliot is in urgent need of life-changing surgery, and Jac is the only person qualified to perform it - but her struggles with anxiety may put his life on the line. Cameron gets a chance to make amends for his past mistakes, but goes about it the wrong way by manipulating a patient's relative. Carole returns from holiday blissfully unaware of what Dom and Lofty are going through(n)

programme.title: Gold Digger
programme.desc: Julia decides not to tell her children she is engaged when they visit at Christmas, leaving Benjamin feeling rejected. However, she ends up letting it slip over dinner - and none of her children are happy for her. Ted angrily questions Benjamin's motives, and cynically proposes to Marsha in an act of oneupmanship. When she spots his selfish reasons and rejects him, it drives him to start drinking again, as well as urging his children to prevent the wedding from going ahead for the sake of their inheritance(n)

programme.title: Spotlight
programme.desc: Investigations into social and political issues affecting Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Plastic Surgery Undressed
programme.desc: Vogue Williams and Mobeen Azhar explain the ins and outs of liposuction, and offer four people considering the procedure a sneak preview of exactly what it entails, by watching a surgeon operate on a sportsman whose physique has suffered following an injury. The very different experiences of two women who have undergone liposuction brings the risks and rewards of the process to light(n)

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: The lively comedian is joined by guests Emma Bunton and Judge Rinder for an evening of entertainment, recorded live in front of an audience at London's Theatre Royal on Drury Lane. Spice Girl and presenter Emma hands over her phone to play Send To All, and two brothers from Coventry are in for the shock of their lives when they find themselves on stage as they become the Unexpected Stars. Plus, Judge Rinder faces a rude awakening in The Midnight Gameshow, and breakthrough singer-songwriter Freya Ridings performs her hit single, Castles(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein examine the case of a fatal shooting on a farm in Staffordshire in 1893, carried out by a 19-year-old poacher(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell explore properties about to be auctioned in London, Manchester and Ulverston, Cumbria, including a house which is across three floors(n)

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: The fake botox treatment that left one woman with life changing injuries. Plus, Michelle Ackerley investigates counterfeit make up products(n)

programme.title: A1: Britain's Longest Road
programme.desc: Traffic officers track down pedestrians walking along a dangerous route alongside the carriageway. Meanwhile, a crash involving two cars sparks fears a driver has worrying injuries(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp oversees proceedings as Tim Weeks and Caroline Hawley help two teams look for items at the South of England Showground in Ardingly, West Sussex(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Adam helps Joe get through a traumatic event. Meanwhile, Sid demonstrates his marketing skills, and a father forces his daughter to confront her feelings of guilt(n)

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Max and Miranda investigate the brutal murder of the famous British author Nicholas Mountford, and investigate the possible link to a bull fighting ban(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a rural property-shopping couple with an £850,000 budget put down roots closer to family in Somerset. Along the way, Jonnie visits Glastonbury and learns how its religious past and strong links to English legend still resonate with people there today(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie and the Rich brothers try to come up with a contemporary cottage garden design in Norwich to please Jane and Terry, who are unhappy with their existing back garden(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Three treasured family heirlooms are brought back to life, a vintage bottle stopper, a cherished peddle scooter, and an engraved powder compact(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Alliance Party(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Matt Baker(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Neil Interviews
programme.desc: The broadcaster interviews Jo Swinson, leader of the Liberal Democrats(n)

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Sheriffs Dave Steele and Michael Perkins visit a man who owes his ex-wife thousands of pounds, leaving her on the verge of bankruptcy. Meanwhile, Rob and Gerald visit a beautiful country house in search of the money owed to a wedding planner who has not received payment, and Billy and Claire challenge the owners of a car dealership(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: As the tenth week of the competition begins, Lord sugar summons the candidates to Burlington House in Central London. Here, the remaining candidates are informed that for this week's task, they have been challenged to create and sell their own perfume, coming up with their own signature scent and design to sell to major retailers. For one team a failure to make decisions proves problematic, while on the other team a bold attempt to make their fragrance stand out does not go to plan. Back in the boardroom, one of the candidates is sent home from the competition(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the midweek Premier League matches, including Liverpool v Everton at Anfield and Manchester United v Tottenham Hotspur at Old Trafford. The Reds went into this match looking to extend their unbeaten run against their Merseyside rivals to 20 matches and further strengthen their title challenge in the process. Meanwhile, United and Spurs were both looking to kickstart their campaigns after disappointing starts to the season. Presented by Gary Lineker, with analysis by Danny Murphy and Jermaine Jenas(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: As Prince Andrew steps back from his public duties, Darragh MacIntyre hears from the victims of is former friend and convicted child sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. The Duke of York has been widely criticised for not expressing sympathy for these women during his television interview with the BBC's Newsnight programme. Virginia Roberts Giuffre claims she was trafficked by Epstein and forced to have sex with the prince, something he denies. In her first interview on British TV, she tells her story and reveals new details about her time with Epstein. Reporter Darragh MacIntyre also looks at another of the duke's friends, Ghislaine Maxwell, and investigates her alleged role in Epstein's prolific sex offending. As the FBI investigation continues, Panorama asks what next in the scandal of Epstein and the prince?(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a selection of camcorder calamities and viewers' mobile phone footage, featuring a spectacular case of stage fright, a shy rhinoceros and a dog with an identity crisis(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, including a birthday girl whose hair catches fire and dogs revealing where they stand on the subject of Europe(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter puts out a fire at a fast-food restaurant and earns the gratitude of the owner, who rewards him with an unlimited supply of free burgers - with disastrous consequences for his health. Animation following the misadventures of America's disaster-prone Griffin family(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter becomes jealous of Lois when she upstages him at a Red Sox fantasy baseball camp. Meanwhile, Stewie runs away from home when Meg is left to babysit him(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg takes advantage of Peter's temporary job as principal of her school to get even with her bullies, while Brian and Stewie open a bed and breakfast(n)

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity: Extra Camp
programme.desc: Companion programme to the reality show, featuring guests and gossip from the jungle(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Percy is stunned when Emily announces she is going to marry Bernard. Tricia is furious when Curly stands her up. Des is annoyed when Alex tells him he is sending him to Newcastle(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mrs Baker calls on Gail and breaks down. Reg is furious when Ken tells him he is not interested in buying the flat. Derek organises a birthday party for Mavis(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Nick is blinded and wanders around looking for Archie. Frank surveys the devastation in the village. Survivors begin to congregate at the Woolpack(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Lynn's house is still on fire. Alan puts together a missing persons list. Shirley arranges a base for the emergency services to work from(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton and Walker investigate a security van raid in Ashfordly and are convinced there is a link to a recent robbery at a local airfield, but a senior officer visiting from Middlesbrough dismisses their claims. Vernon takes a sudden interest in the flower show when he discovers David might be the owner of a rare, potentially prize-winning fuchsia and turns to Blaketon and Ventress for help when the plant suddenly disappears(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: The depot football team are promised substantial cash bonuses if they can win their match against the Basildon Bashers. However, Stan manages to injure his own side's star player and a surprise lies in store when they meet their opponents. Reg Varney stars(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: The company mounts an advertising campaign to attract more passengers, and launches a contest to find the perfect bus driver. Reg Varney stars(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Jo are seeking an additional female flatmate, so Robin has to get in touch with his feminine side. The first episode of the 1970s comedy series, with Richard O'Sullivan, Paula Wilcox and Sally Thomsett(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan as the genteel sleuth. A woman experiences an unsettling vision after moving into the home of her dreams - and it transpires she lived in the house as a child. Miss Marple investigates the history of the property and discovers it was connected with a baffling disappearance years before - which has never been solved. With Dawn French, Russ Abbot, Geraldine Chaplin, Phil Davis and Sarah Parish(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Colonel and Joan Maltravers host a dinner party to celebrate their son's return from Kenya but the meal is disrupted by an armed robbery. David gives chase to the thief and becomes a local hero - much to the exasperation of Bernie. Liz has a difficult decision to make after receiving an exciting offer from Ben. Guest starring Simon Williams, his wife Lucy Fleming and their son Tam Williams(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank tells Chris he is going to be helicoptered out of the village, Eric still cannot find Elizabeth, and there is some grave news for the Sugden family(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Annie is still in a coma, Elsa finds out that Nick is in hospital but there is no sign of Alice, and Bernard is told there are still nine people unaccounted for(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken tells Reg he would move if given a financial incentive, Bernard is concerned about Percy's feelings regarding him marrying Emily, and Derek throws a party for Mavis(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken takes Denise walking in the country, Reg is thrilled to accept an offer on his flat, and Bernard is pleased when at last Percy wishes him well with Emily(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster teams up with a dashing man to investigate a series of poison-pen letters in the sleepy village of Lymstock. Two of the missives have led to the deaths of Mona Symmington, the wife of Lymstock solicitor Richard, and Agnes Woddell, the parlour maid at the Symmingtons' house. However, Miss Marple suspects there is more to these deaths than meets the eye. Murder mystery with an all-star cast, featuring Geraldine McEwan, James D'Arcy, Emilia Fox, Imogen Stubbs, Ken Russell, Harry Enfield and Kelly Brook(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Colonel and Joan Maltravers host a dinner party to celebrate their son's return from Kenya but the meal is disrupted by an armed robbery. David gives chase to the thief and becomes a local hero - much to the exasperation of Bernie. Liz has a difficult decision to make after receiving an exciting offer from Ben. Guest starring Simon Williams, his wife Lucy Fleming and their son Tam Williams(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A well-known eccentric contacts the Belgian sleuth when he is haunted by suicidal nightmares. However, he fails to follow Poirot's advice, and is found dead soon afterwards. Drama, starring David Suchet, with Joely Richardson and Mary Tamm(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: A secret admirer makes Ruth feel a little uncomfortable when he places a red rose on her car windscreen and then leaves a birthday cake on her kitchen table while she's at the radio station, and when matters take a turn for the worse, Martin has no choice but to take drastic action. Meanwhile, Bert is furious when one of Louisa's pupils writes a damning review of his restaurant in the school newspaper, and a new pharmacist arrives in the village. Annabelle Apsion (Shameless) joins the cast(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Shaun Evans steps into the shoes of John Thaw in this 1965-set prequel to Inspector Morse, playing a younger version of the cerebral detective involved in the hunt for a missing schoolgirl. Soon deep in a full-blown murder investigation, Endeavour is sidelined, discredited and at a dead end - so he risks all to begin his own quest for justice. Roger Allam and Charlie Creed-Miles co-star in the mystery(n)

programme.title: Safe House
programme.desc: The drama returns with a new cast, location and story. Former detective Tom Brook hears news of a shocking abduction, convincing him that a killer he once investigated has returned. Despite the investigating officer's initial doubts, the victim's family are soon placed under Tom's protection in the safe house Tom runs as a business with his partner Sam. Thriller, starring Stephen Moyer, Zoe Tapper, Ashley Walters, Jason Watkins and Sunetra Sarker(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack returns to duty after a period of suspension, and finds himself investigating the murder of a man who had been leading a double life. In addition, he also has to deal with the baffling case of a man found dead with his right foot hacked off and replaced by a severed left foot. Drama, starring David Jason and Amanda Root(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Blakey organises an exhibition as the bus company celebrates its half-centenary. Stan reminisces, and on falling asleep finds himself at the wheel of a bus from the turn of the 20th century. Comedy. starring Reg Varney and Stephen Lewis(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan is offered a job in a car factory in the midlands and decides the best way to quit his job at the bus depot is by getting himself sacked in order to earn an extra week's wages. Reg Varney stars(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: The housemates are put out when Chrissy's mother arrives for an unscheduled visit. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster teams up with a dashing man to investigate a series of poison-pen letters in the sleepy village of Lymstock. Two of the missives have led to the deaths of Mona Symmington, the wife of Lymstock solicitor Richard, and Agnes Woddell, the parlour maid at the Symmingtons' house. However, Miss Marple suspects there is more to these deaths than meets the eye. Murder mystery with an all-star cast, featuring Geraldine McEwan, James D'Arcy, Emilia Fox, Imogen Stubbs, Ken Russell, Harry Enfield and Kelly Brook(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The police investigate the murder of an ex-army recluse and archaeological enthusiast and suspect a building dispute could provide a motive, while Dennis asks Liz for advice when he begins to notice a change in Jenny's behaviour. Vernon decides to play Cupid on behalf of his relative Gareth and sets him up on a blind date with Rosie, but does not count on the mechanic having another admirer. Rural drama starring Duncan Bell, Aislin McGuckin and Vanessa Hehir(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The emergency services continue the search for Alice, Zoe is worried about Frank when she sees that Kim is staying at Home Farm, and Michael and Eric row about Elizabeth(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris is grateful that the crash brought Kathy and him back together, and Zoe warns Kim not to mess Frank around. The village children struggle emotionally with what has happened(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken and Denise have a wonderful night together, Percy assures Maureen he is not after Maud's money, and Mavis gets upset with gossip about Derek taking money from kids(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mike gets an unexpected tax demand, and Alma refuses to let him touch her money. Percy and Maud go on an OAP outing, and Reg roots through her belongings in search of a will(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster meets fellow enthusiasts Tommy and Tuppence at a nursing home, where the ramblings of an elderly resident prompt them to join forces and investigate the unsolved mystery of a murdered child. The trail leads to the Norfolk village of Farrell St Edmund, a community harbouring sinister secrets and tales of heartbreak. Geraldine McEwan heads an all-star cast, including Greta Scacchi, Anthony Andrews, Charles Dance, June Whitfield and Claire Bloom(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The police investigate the murder of an ex-army recluse and archaeological enthusiast and suspect a building dispute could provide a motive, while Dennis asks Liz for advice when he begins to notice a change in Jenny's behaviour. Vernon decides to play Cupid on behalf of his relative Gareth and sets him up on a blind date with Rosie, but does not count on the mechanic having another admirer. Rural drama starring Duncan Bell, Aislin McGuckin and Vanessa Hehir(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Part one of two. While holidaying in Cornwall with his friend Captain Hastings, the sleuth meets a local woman who has seemingly become unaccountably accident-prone - but soon starts to suspect she is actually a victim of foul play. David Suchet and Hugh Fraser star(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa wants Martin to engage more with James, so she arranges for him to take their baby to playgroup, where he has to pair up with a mother and daughter and sing songs. Meanwhile, Al signs up to a dating website, Bert is suspicious when he catches Jennifer in the bathroom with a needle, and Ruth moves into a cottage in the village, but soon has trouble with a grumpy new neighbour (Ronald Pickup)(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: When a respectable GP is found dead in a public lavatory, the young Morse's outlandish theories see him relegated to general duties by the newly arrived Chief Superintendent Bright. Humiliated and humbled, Endeavour has to investigate from the sidelines, but it's not long before he's questioning DI Fred Thursday's orders and risking his career to avert a family tragedy. Shaun Evans stars, with Roger Allam, Anton Lesser, Sean Rigby and James Bradshaw(n)

programme.title: Safe House
programme.desc: As the search for Julie intensifies, John's erratic behaviour in the safe house soon causes Tom and Sam to become intensely suspicious. They are surprised that he is continuing to run his business under such traumatic circumstances - and it also becomes clear that Dani is wary of her mother's boyfriend. Meanwhile, when Tom's former boss Elizabeth turns up looking for answers, Sam is left wondering what the real reasons were for her partner leaving the police. Thriller, starring Stephen Moyer, Zoe Tapper, Ashley Walters and Dervla Kirwan(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth helps an old friend find refuge at country house Greenshaw's Folly, but a sinister shadow falls over the strange building's various inhabitants and a murderer strikes. Miss Marple uncovers secrets from the past about Miss Greenshaw as a dangerous storm gathers and the bodies start to pile up. Julia McKenzie heads an all-star cast that includes Jim Moir (better known as Vic Reeves), Kimberley Nixon, Fiona Shaw, Robert Glenister, Julia Sawalha, Judy Parfitt and Matt Willis (formerly of pop group Busted)(n)

programme.desc: The depot football team are promised substantial cash bonuses if they can win their match against the Basildon Bashers. However, Stan manages to injure his own side's star player and a surprise lies in store when they meet their opponents. Reg Varney stars(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Jack and the inspector try to fix a water heater, but their efforts end in disaster. Comedy, starring Stephen Lewis and Bob Grant(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: The inspector's mother comes to stay, and proves to be a thoroughly unpleasant person. Seventies comedy, starring Stephen Lewis and Bob Grant(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy wonders whether she is desirable, and Robin's lack of response leaves her downhearted. Comedy, starring Paula Wilcox and Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster meets fellow enthusiasts Tommy and Tuppence at a nursing home, where the ramblings of an elderly resident prompt them to join forces and investigate the unsolved mystery of a murdered child. The trail leads to the Norfolk village of Farrell St Edmund, a community harbouring sinister secrets and tales of heartbreak. Geraldine McEwan heads an all-star cast, including Greta Scacchi, Anthony Andrews, Charles Dance, June Whitfield and Claire Bloom(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton investigates the murder of a retired policeman who was about to flee the village for Spain and, after visiting the victim's widow, discovers the man kept more than a few secrets from his wife. Vernon decides to arrange a poker night in the hope it will make him easy money, but doesn't count on playing against a hustler. Rural drama starring Duncan Bell, Geoffrey Hughes and Mark Jordon(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The village is in a solemn mood as the day of the funerals arrives, Rachel blames Joe for Mark's death, and Elsa tells Eric that she is going for custody of Alice(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The hillside is covered with flowers and people paying their respects, Turner and Caroline worry about Nick's state of mind, and Alan has a surprise for Shirley(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Audrey tries to find out all the gossip about Ken and Denise, Don worries that she will tell Barlow about the phone calls, and Mike throws his money around at the pub(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maureen gets fed up with Reg going on about wills, Bernard quizzes people about Emily's breakdown, and Rita realises Sally is worried about the money(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A blizzard forces the sleuthing spinster to spend a night in isolated Sittaford House, situated on a Devon moor. Without explanation, the building's owner, up-and-coming politician Clive Trevelyan, decides to spend the evening in a local hotel - only for a Ouija board to predict his murder. Mystery drama with an all-star cast, including Geraldine McEwan, Robert Hardy, Matthew Kelly, Patricia Hodge and Mel Smith(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton investigates the murder of a retired policeman who was about to flee the village for Spain and, after visiting the victim's widow, discovers the man kept more than a few secrets from his wife. Vernon decides to arrange a poker night in the hope it will make him easy money, but doesn't count on playing against a hustler. Rural drama starring Duncan Bell, Geoffrey Hughes and Mark Jordon(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Belgian sleuth's Cornish holiday comes to a dramatic conclusion as he unravels the mystery behind the series of disastrous near-misses that placed the life of a beautiful heiress in danger, and as the truth comes to light, a secret engagement is revealed. David Suchet stars, with Philip Jackson and Hugh Fraser(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Obsessive-compulsive Mrs Tishell returns to the village, but her affection for Martin quickly disappears when he refuses to pass her fit for work. As if he didn't have enough on his plate, the grumpy doctor must also face his fear of blood when tests show that Lorna has a high level of ferritin, which is placing a strain on her heart and liver. Meanwhile, PC Penhale hopes to be selected for an elite police squad by taking part in a survival training course - but ends up shooting himself in the foot(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Morse meets his intellectual match when a serial killer haunting the streets of Oxford keeps himself one step ahead of the law and leaves cryptic messages to goad the police. At first glance, it seems to be the work of a deranged lunatic, but the elaborate staging of the crimes suggests method in the madness - and a macabre sharing of the sleuth's passion for opera. Starring Shaun Evans, with Roger Allam, Anton Lesser, Sean Rigby and James Bradshaw(n)

programme.title: Safe House
programme.desc: Tom makes a huge breakthrough in the case when he links shady estate agent Roger Lane to a number of locations related to the previous abductions. Jane arrests Lane and grills him on his whereabouts - and Simon Duke even picks him out of an identity parade. Have they finally got their man? Meanwhile, after Dani gets herself into trouble, the shocking reason for her mistrust of John is finally revealed. Crime thriller, starring Stephen Moyer, Sunetra Sarker, Jason Watkins, Ashley Walters and Sacha Parkinson(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple

David gives chase to the thief and becomes a local hero - much to the exasperation of Bernie. Liz has a difficult decision to make after receiving an exciting offer from Ben. Guest starring Simon Williams, his wife Lucy Fleming and their son Tam Williams(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Blakey organises an exhibition as the bus company celebrates its half-centenary. Stan reminisces, and on falling asleep finds himself at the wheel of a bus from the turn of the 20th century. Comedy. starring Reg Varney and Stephen Lewis(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan is offered a job in a car factory in the midlands and decides the best way to quit his job at the bus depot is by getting himself sacked in order to earn an extra week's wages. Reg Varney stars(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: The housemates are put out when Chrissy's mother arrives for an unscheduled visit. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster teams up with a dashing man to investigate a series of poison-pen letters in the sleepy village of Lymstock. Two of the missives have led to the deaths of Mona Symmington, the wife of Lymstock solicitor Richard, and Agnes Woddell, the parlour maid at the Symmingtons' house. However, Miss Marple suspects there is more to these deaths than meets the eye. Murder mystery with an all-star cast, featuring Geraldine McEwan, James D'Arcy, Emilia Fox, Imogen Stubbs, Ken Russell, Harry Enfield and Kelly Brook(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The police investigate the murder of an ex-army recluse and archaeological enthusiast and suspect a building dispute could provide a motive, while Dennis asks Liz for advice when he begins to notice a change in Jenny's behaviour. Vernon decides to play Cupid on behalf of his relative Gareth and sets him up on a blind date with Rosie, but does not count on the mechanic having another admirer. Rural drama starring Duncan Bell, Aislin McGuckin and Vanessa Hehir(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The emergency services continue the search for Alice, Zoe is worried about Frank when she sees that Kim is staying at Home Farm, and Michael and Eric row about Elizabeth(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris is grateful that the crash brought Kathy and him back together, and Zoe warns Kim not to mess Frank around. The village children struggle emotionally with what has happened(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken and Denise have a wonderful night together, Percy assures Maureen he is not after Maud's money, and Mavis gets upset with gossip about Derek taking money from kids(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mike gets an unexpected tax demand, and Alma refuses to let him touch her money. Percy and Maud go on an OAP outing, and Reg roots through her belongings in search of a will(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster meets fellow enthusiasts Tommy and Tuppence at a nursing home, where the ramblings of an elderly resident prompt them to join forces and investigate the unsolved mystery of a murdered child. The trail leads to the Norfolk village of Farrell St Edmund, a community harbouring sinister secrets and tales of heartbreak. Geraldine McEwan heads an all-star cast, including Greta Scacchi, Anthony Andrews, Charles Dance, June Whitfield and Claire Bloom(n)

programme.desc: Part one of two. While holidaying in Cornwall with his friend Captain Hastings, the sleuth meets a local woman who has seemingly become unaccountably accident-prone - but soon starts to suspect she is actually a victim of foul play. David Suchet and Hugh Fraser star(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa wants Martin to engage more with James, so she arranges for him to take their baby to playgroup, where he has to pair up with a mother and daughter and sing songs. Meanwhile, Al signs up to a dating website, Bert is suspicious when he catches Jennifer in the bathroom with a needle, and Ruth moves into a cottage in the village, but soon has trouble with a grumpy new neighbour (Ronald Pickup)(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: When a respectable GP is found dead in a public lavatory, the young Morse's outlandish theories see him relegated to general duties by the newly arrived Chief Superintendent Bright. Humiliated and humbled, Endeavour has to investigate from the sidelines, but it's not long before he's questioning DI Fred Thursday's orders and risking his career to avert a family tragedy. Shaun Evans stars, with Roger Allam, Anton Lesser, Sean Rigby and James Bradshaw(n)

programme.title: Safe House
programme.desc: As the search for Julie intensifies, John's erratic behaviour in the safe house soon causes Tom and Sam to become intensely suspicious. They are surprised that he is continuing to run his business under such traumatic circumstances - and it also becomes clear that Dani is wary of her mother's boyfriend. Meanwhile, when Tom's former boss Elizabeth turns up looking for answers, Sam is left wondering what the real reasons were for her partner leaving the police. Thriller, starring Stephen Moyer, Zoe Tapper, Ashley Walters and Dervla Kirwan(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth helps an old friend find refuge at country house Greenshaw's Folly, but a sinister shadow falls over the strange building's various inhabitants and a murderer strikes. Miss Marple uncovers secrets from the past about Miss Greenshaw as a dangerous storm gathers and the bodies start to pile up. Julia McKenzie heads an all-star cast that includes Jim Moir (better known as Vic Reeves), Kimberley Nixon, Fiona Shaw, Robert Glenister, Julia Sawalha, Judy Parfitt and Matt Willis (formerly of pop group Busted)(n)

programme.desc: The depot football team are promised substantial cash bonuses if they can win their match against the Basildon Bashers. However, Stan manages to injure his own side's star player and a surprise lies in store when they meet their opponents. Reg Varney stars(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Audrey tries to find out all the gossip about Ken and Denise, Don worries that she will tell Barlow about the phone calls, and Mike throws his money around at the pub(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maureen gets fed up with Reg going on about wills, Bernard quizzes people about Emily's breakdown, and Rita realises Sally is worried about the money(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A blizzard forces the sleuthing spinster to spend a night in isolated Sittaford House, situated on a Devon moor. Without explanation, the building's owner, up-and-coming politician Clive Trevelyan, decides to spend the evening in a local hotel - only for a Ouija board to predict his murder. Mystery drama with an all-star cast, including Geraldine McEwan, Robert Hardy, Matthew Kelly, Patricia Hodge and Mel Smith(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton investigates the murder of a retired policeman who was about to flee the village for Spain and, after visiting the victim's widow, discovers the man kept more than a few secrets from his wife. Vernon decides to arrange a poker night in the hope it will make him easy money, but doesn't count on playing against a hustler. Rural drama starring Duncan Bell, Geoffrey Hughes and Mark Jordon(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Belgian sleuth's Cornish holiday comes to a dramatic conclusion as he unravels the mystery behind the series of disastrous near-misses that placed the life of a beautiful heiress in danger, and as the truth comes to light, a secret engagement is revealed. David Suchet stars, with Philip Jackson and Hugh Fraser(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Obsessive-compulsive Mrs Tishell returns to the village, but her affection for Martin quickly disappears when he refuses to pass her fit for work. As if he didn't have enough on his plate, the grumpy doctor must also face his fear of blood when tests show that Lorna has a high level of ferritin, which is placing a strain on her heart and liver. Meanwhile, PC Penhale hopes to be selected for an elite police squad by taking part in a survival training course - but ends up shooting himself in the foot(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Morse meets his intellectual match when a serial killer haunting the streets of Oxford keeps himself one step ahead of the law and leaves cryptic messages to goad the police. At first glance, it seems to be the work of a deranged lunatic, but the elaborate staging of the crimes suggests method in the madness - and a macabre sharing of the sleuth's passion for opera. Starring Shaun Evans, with Roger Allam, Anton Lesser, Sean Rigby and James Bradshaw(n)

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: Paul is kidnapped and Harry and the Contessa are forced to assist in a theft in order to save him. Drama, guest starring Peter Bowles, with Robert Vaughn, Nyree Dawn Porter and Tony Anholt(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar visits Venice to save a damsel in distress - only to find himself plunged headlong into a seedy underworld of gangsters, intrigue and deadly revenge. Roger Moore plays the suave secret agent, with William Sylvester, Patrick Troughton and Lois Maxwell(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: An obsessive man develops a fixation on his neighbour, who is recovering from depression, and murders anyone he sees as a threat to her - including her latest boyfriend(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The revelation that a holistic doctor has been using alternative therapy to treat ailments, and may have been responsible for the death of a patient, presents Quincy with a dilemma - and gives him ample food for thought. Jack Klugman and John S Ragin star(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur makes a hasty agreement to stage a burglary after his tailor's staff go on strike demanding more pay. The bumbling safecracker he hires blows himself up, leaving Arthur and Terry with the task of liberating him from hospital before his bandages are removed and the duo can be linked with the botched crime(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: An apparently respected gynaecologist's suitcase is stolen, but he is reluctant to reveal its contents. On further investigation the squad learns he was in the process of smuggling untaxed money out of the country and that a glamorous Latin woman with criminal connections, may hold the key to its whereabouts(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are trained to act as bodyguards to a Middle Eastern president who is preparing to sign an important peace treaty. Meanwhile, two killers are receiving similar training to prepare them to assassinate him. As their paths cross, the CI5 operatives learn the would-be killers have some useful inside information and share more in common with them than they may have imagined(n)

programme.desc: Lydia, a successful business consultant, thrives on organising the lives of others, but even her professional ambitions are forced to take a back seat when she meets Robert, a widowed toy inventor struggling to balance work commitments with his duties as a father.
